title: Hugo's Soccer Goal
synopsis: Hugo is nervous about trying out for his school's soccer team, but with encouragement from friends and teachers, he discovers that practice, teamwork, and believing in yourself can help you achieve your dreams.

chapters.title: The Big Announcement
chapters.content:
Hugo sat at his desk in Mrs. Garcia's third-grade classroom, tapping his pencil nervously. The morning sun streamed through the windows, making everything feel bright and hopeful. But Hugo didn't feel very hopeful at all.

"Class, I have an exciting announcement," Mrs. Garcia said with a big smile. She walked to the front of the room and picked up a colorful flyer from her desk. "Our school is starting a soccer team this year! Coach Martinez will be holding tryouts next Friday after school."

Hugo's heart jumped. He loved soccer more than anything in the world. At home, he kicked his soccer ball around the backyard every day after homework. He dreamed of scoring amazing goals and playing on a real team with real uniforms.

But there was one big problem. Hugo had never played on a team before. What if he wasn't good enough? What if the other kids were much better than him?

"Who wants to sign up?" Mrs. Garcia asked, holding up a clipboard.

Several hands shot up around the classroom. Hugo wanted to raise his hand so badly, but it felt stuck to his desk. His best friend Sam, who sat next to him, immediately put his hand in the air.

"I'm definitely trying out!" Sam whispered to Hugo excitedly. "You should too! We could be on the team together!"

Hugo nodded weakly, but inside his stomach was doing flip-flops. He watched as kids walked up to sign their names on the list. Each name on that paper meant more competition. Each name meant someone who might be better than him.

When the clipboard finally reached Hugo's desk, he stared at the blank lines. His pencil hovered over the paper for what felt like forever. Finally, he passed it to the next person without writing anything.

Sam looked surprised. "Aren't you going to sign up?"

"Maybe," Hugo said quietly. "I need to think about it."

But deep down, Hugo was afraid he already knew his answer.

illustrations.description: A chalkboard style drawing showing Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes sitting at a school desk looking nervous, surrounded by mathematical equations and school doodles drawn in white chalk on a dark green board
illustrations.alt: Hugo sitting nervously at his school desk

illustrations.alt: Mrs. Garcia announcing soccer tryouts to the class

chapters.title: Playground Practice
chapters.content:
At recess, Hugo sat on the swings while Sam and some other kids played soccer on the grass field. He watched them run back and forth, laughing and cheering when someone made a good pass or kicked the ball far.

"Come play with us, Hugo!" Sam called out, waving him over.

Hugo shook his head. "I'm okay here!"

But he wasn't really okay. He wanted to join them so badly it hurt. He imagined himself running onto the field, dribbling the ball like a pro, and scoring the winning goal. In his daydreams, everyone cheered for him.

After a few minutes, Sam jogged over to the swings. He was breathing hard and had grass stains on his knees, but he looked happy.

"Why won't you come play?" Sam asked, sitting on the swing next to Hugo. "You're really good at soccer. I've seen you practice in your backyard."

Hugo kicked at the dirt with his sneaker. "That's different. That's just by myself. Playing with other people is harder."

"But that's what makes it fun!" Sam said. "And you'll never get better if you don't practice with other kids."

Just then, the soccer ball rolled toward them. Maria, a girl from their class, ran over to get it.

"Hi Hugo!" she said cheerfully. "Want to kick the ball back to us?"

Hugo looked at the black and white soccer ball sitting right in front of him. It seemed to be waiting for him. Before he could think too much about it, he stood up and gave it a gentle kick.

The ball sailed perfectly to where the other kids were standing. They cheered and gave him a thumbs up.

"Nice kick!" Maria called back.

Hugo felt a little spark of confidence. Maybe he wasn't as bad as he thought. But was he good enough for a real team? That was still the big question.

illustrations.alt: Hugo watching other kids play soccer from the swings

illustrations.alt: Hugo kicking the soccer ball to his classmates

chapters.title: Encouragement from Coach Martinez
chapters.content:
The next day during lunch, Hugo was walking to the cafeteria when he saw a tall man with a whistle around his neck putting up soccer posters in the hallway. The man had kind eyes and a friendly smile.

"Excuse me," the man said to Hugo. "Could you help me hold this poster straight?"

Hugo nodded and held one corner of the poster while the man taped it to the wall. It showed a picture of kids playing soccer with big smiles on their faces. At the bottom it said, "Join the Riverside Elementary Soccer Team!"

"Thanks for the help," the man said. "I'm Coach Martinez. Are you planning to try out for the team?"

Hugo's cheeks turned a little red. "I... I'm not sure. I don't know if I'm good enough."

Coach Martinez crouched down so he was at Hugo's eye level. "Can I tell you a secret? When I was your age, I was afraid to try out for my school's soccer team too."

"Really?" Hugo asked, surprised. Coach Martinez looked like someone who had never been afraid of anything.

"Really," Coach Martinez said with a nod. "I thought all the other kids would be better than me. But you know what I learned? The only way to find out what you can do is to try. And even if you don't make the team this year, you'll learn something that will help you next year."

Hugo thought about this. "But what if I embarrass myself?"

"Everyone makes mistakes when they're learning," Coach Martinez said. "That's how we get better. The bravest thing you can do is try, even when you're scared."

Coach Martinez stood up and handed Hugo a flyer. "Think about it, okay? Tryouts are Friday at 3:30. We'll spend time teaching skills too, not just testing what you already know."

As Hugo walked to the cafeteria, he folded the flyer carefully and put it in his pocket. Maybe Coach Martinez was right. Maybe being brave meant trying even when you were scared.

illustrations.description: A chalkboard style drawing showing Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes helping Coach Martinez hang a soccer poster in the school hallway, with chalk-drawn equations and school lockers sketched around the dark green board
illustrations.alt: Hugo helping Coach Martinez put up a soccer poster

illustrations.alt: Coach Martinez encouraging Hugo about soccer tryouts

chapters.title: A Friend's Support
chapters.content:
That evening, Hugo sat at his kitchen table doing homework, but he couldn't concentrate. The soccer flyer was right next to his math worksheet, and he kept looking at it. His mom noticed him staring at the paper.

"What's that, sweetie?" she asked, sitting down next to him.

Hugo showed her the flyer. "It's for soccer tryouts at school. I want to try out, but I'm nervous."

Mom smiled and ruffled his hair. "You love soccer. I see you practicing in the backyard every day. What are you nervous about?"

"What if I'm not as good as the other kids? What if I don't make the team and everyone laughs at me?"

Mom wrapped her arm around Hugo's shoulders. "You know what I think? I think you're braver than you realize. And I think you'll regret it more if you don't try than if you do try and it doesn't work out."

The next morning at school, Sam was waiting for Hugo by the classroom door. He looked excited about something.

"Guess what?" Sam said. "I talked to Maria and Jake yesterday. They're both nervous about tryouts too! Maria said she's never played goalie before, but she wants to learn. And Jake said he's worried he's not fast enough."

Hugo felt a little better knowing he wasn't the only one who was nervous. "Really?"

"Really! And you know what else? I asked Coach Martinez if friends could try out together, and he said yes. We could all support each other!"

Sam pulled out a pen and the soccer signup sheet that Mrs. Garcia had left on her desk. "Come on, Hugo. Let's sign up together. It'll be more fun with friends."

Hugo looked at the sheet, then at Sam's encouraging face, then at the sheet again. He thought about what his mom had said and what Coach Martinez had told him. Maybe being brave meant taking a deep breath and writing his name, even though his hand was shaking a little.

Slowly, Hugo wrote "Hugo Peterson" on the line next to Sam's name.

"Yes!" Sam cheered quietly, so he wouldn't disturb the other classes. "This is going to be awesome!"

Hugo hoped Sam was right.

illustrations.description: A chalkboard style drawing showing Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes sitting at a kitchen table with homework and the soccer flyer while his mom talks to him supportively, surrounded by chalk-drawn mathematical equations and school supplies on the dark green board
illustrations.alt: Hugo discussing soccer tryouts with his mom at home

illustrations.description: A chalkboard style drawing showing Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes writing his name on the soccer signup sheet while Sam encourages him, with white chalk school doodles and equations decorating the dark green board
illustrations.alt: Hugo signing up for soccer tryouts with Sam's encouragement

chapters.title: The Big Day
chapters.content:
Friday afternoon arrived faster than Hugo expected. All day long, his stomach felt like it was full of jumping beans. During math class, he kept thinking about soccer drills. During reading time, the words on the page kept turning into little soccer balls in his imagination.

When the final bell rang, Hugo packed his backpack extra slowly. Sam had to wait for him by the classroom door.

"Come on, Hugo! We need to go change into our gym clothes!" Sam said excitedly.

In the locker room, Hugo changed into his shorts and t-shirt. His hands were shaking a little as he tied his sneakers. Around him, other kids were chatting nervously about the tryouts.

"I heard Coach Martinez is really nice," said Maria, who was tying her hair back in a ponytail.

"My older brother said he focuses on teamwork more than just being the best player," added Jake.

Hugo felt a little better hearing this, but he was still nervous. What if he forgot how to kick the ball? What if he tripped and fell in front of everyone?

When they walked out to the soccer field, Hugo saw about fifteen other kids gathered around Coach Martinez. Some of them looked confident and excited. Others looked just as nervous as Hugo felt.

"Welcome, everyone!" Coach Martinez said with a big smile. "I'm so excited to see all of you here today. Before we start any drills, I want you to know something important. Today isn't about being perfect. It's about showing effort, having fun, and working together as a team."

Hugo took a deep breath. Maybe this wouldn't be so scary after all.

"Let's start with something easy," Coach Martinez continued. "We're going to pass the ball in a circle. Focus on making a good pass to your teammate, and don't worry if it's not perfect."

As the kids formed a circle, Hugo found himself between Sam and Maria. The soccer ball came around the circle, getting closer and closer to him. When Maria passed it to him, Hugo felt his heart beating fast. But then he remembered all those hours practicing in his backyard. He took a breath, focused on Sam, and made a gentle pass.

The ball rolled right to Sam's feet. "Nice pass, Hugo!" Sam said.

Hugo smiled. Maybe he could do this after all.

illustrations.description: A chalkboard style drawing showing Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes nervously tying his sneakers in the school locker room with other kids getting ready for soccer tryouts, chalk equations and school sketches around the dark green board edges
illustrations.alt: Hugo getting ready for soccer tryouts in the locker room

illustrations.description: A chalkboard style drawing showing Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes passing a soccer ball to Sam in a circle of kids on the soccer field, with Coach Martinez watching, white chalk school doodles on the dark green board
illustrations.alt: Hugo making his first successful pass during soccer tryouts

chapters.title: Hugo Finds His Courage
chapters.content:
As the tryout continued, Coach Martinez had the kids do different activities. They practiced dribbling the ball around orange cones, which Hugo did pretty well. They practiced kicking the ball into the goal, and Hugo made two out of five shots. Most importantly, they scrimmaged in small teams, which meant they got to play a real mini soccer game.

Hugo was on a team with Sam, Maria, and two other kids he didn't know very well. At first, he hung back and let the others run toward the ball. But then something happened that changed everything.

The ball rolled toward Hugo, and without thinking too much about it, he started dribbling it toward the goal. One of the kids from the other team tried to take the ball away, but Hugo remembered a move he'd practiced in his backyard. He gently tapped the ball to the left and ran around the other player.

"Go, Hugo!" he heard Sam shout from behind him.

Suddenly, Hugo was in front of the goal with just the goalie between him and scoring. His heart was pounding, but this time it wasn't from fear. It was from excitement. He pulled his leg back and kicked the ball as hard as he could.

The ball flew past the goalie and into the net!

"GOAL!" everyone cheered, including the kids on the other team.

Hugo couldn't believe it. He had scored a goal! In a real game! With other people watching!

Coach Martinez blew his whistle. "Great job, Hugo! I could see you thinking about that play and using good strategy."

After the scrimmage ended, all the kids gathered around Coach Martinez for his final words.

"You all did wonderfully today," he said. "I saw great effort, good teamwork, and most importantly, kids supporting each other. I'm happy to tell you that everyone who tried out today has earned a spot on our team!"

Hugo's mouth dropped open. Everyone made the team? Including him?

"Our first practice is next Tuesday after school," Coach Martinez continued. "I'll see you all then!"

As the kids started to leave the field, Sam ran over to Hugo with a huge grin. "You scored a goal! That was amazing!"

"I can't believe I did that," Hugo said, still feeling amazed.

"I can," said Maria, joining them. "You were great out there. I'm so glad we're all going to be on the team together."

Walking home with Sam, Hugo felt like he was floating on air. He had been so scared to try out, but look what had happened! He'd made new friends, scored a goal, and earned a spot on the soccer team.

"You know what the best part is?" Hugo said to Sam.

"What?"

"I almost didn't sign up because I was too scared. But if I hadn't tried, I never would have known what I could do."

Sam nodded. "Sometimes the best things happen when we're brave enough to try something new."

Hugo smiled and kicked a small stone down the sidewalk like it was a soccer ball. He couldn't wait to tell his mom about his goal, and he couldn't wait for the team's first practice. Most of all, he couldn't wait to see what other amazing things might happen when he was brave enough to try.

questions.question: Why was Hugo nervous about signing up for soccer tryouts?

questions.question: Who encouraged Hugo to think about trying out for the soccer team?
questions.explanation: Coach Martinez talked to him in the hallway, Mrs. Garcia announced the tryouts, and his mom encouraged him at home.

questions.question: What did Hugo's mom tell him about being brave?
choices: You'll regret not trying more than trying and failing
Only try things you know you'll succeed at
Wait until you're older to try new things
questions.explanation: Hugo's mom told him he would regret it more if he didn't try than if he tried and it didn't work out.

choices: Learning that his friends were also nervous
Coach Martinez promising he'd make the team
questions.explanation: When Sam told Hugo that Maria and Jake were also nervous about tryouts, Hugo felt better knowing he wasn't the only one who felt scared.

The coach told him to slow down

Everyone who tried out made the team
They had to wait a week to find out
